name: Rapid Credit Boosters
description_short: San Diego credit repair firm offering dispute services, tradeline placement, and credit coaching for individuals and businesses since 2015.
description_long:
Rapid Credit Boosters is a for-profit credit repair LLC founded in 2015 and headquartered in San Diego, California. The company was established by Antoine Sallis, who has cultivated a media presence through features in Respect Magazine and associations with influencer Billy Carson. The company serves clients nationwide, though it carries no publicly disclosed NFCC, HUD, or CDFI certifications, and is not accredited by the Better Business Bureau.

The company's core offering is a combination of credit dispute services and tradeline placement. On the dispute side, they work to remove hard inquiries, collections, and negative items from clients' credit reports. Tradeline placement — adding clients as authorized users on seasoned credit accounts — is also a primary service, as is credit counseling aimed at educating clients on responsible credit usage. Beyond score repair, they specialize in goal-oriented credit optimization: helping clients qualify for auto loans, mortgages, high-limit credit cards, and startup or small business funding. Pricing is not publicly disclosed; fees are only revealed through a free initial consultation.

What distinguishes Rapid Credit Boosters is its positioning as a relationship-driven, personalized service rather than a high-volume automated platform. The company claims clients can see credit score improvements within 30 days, and its Google rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars across 104 reviews suggests a meaningful base of satisfied clients. Antoine Sallis's public profile — appearing in entrepreneurial media and aligning with prominent influencers — gives the brand a distinctive personality-led identity uncommon among credit repair firms.

The honest assessment here requires flagging several material concerns. The company uses a Gmail address (rapidcreditboosters@gmail.com) rather than a business domain, which is unusual for a professional financial services firm. More significantly, Yelp listed the business as closed as of January 2026, raising genuine questions about current operational status. Tradeline placement, one of their featured services, is a legally gray practice that the CFPB has scrutinized. Prospective clients should verify the company is still operating before engaging and request a clear written fee agreement before starting any services.
pros: 4.8/5 Google rating from 104 reviews — strong volume of positive client feedback
Founder Antoine Sallis has verifiable media presence (Respect Magazine, Billy Carson association)
Covers both personal and business credit needs under one service
Goal-oriented approach: clients work toward specific outcomes like car loans, mortgages, or business funding
Offers credit counseling alongside repair — not purely dispute-filing
Free consultation available before any financial commitment
Over a decade in operation since founding in 2015
cons: Yelp lists the business as closed as of January 2026 — current operational status is unverified
No pricing transparency; fees are only disclosed after a consultation
Uses a Gmail address (rapidcreditboosters@gmail.com) rather than a professional business domain
Not BBB accredited, and specific BBB letter grade is unconfirmed
Tradeline placement, a core service, is a legally gray practice subject to CFPB scrutiny
best_for: Individuals with collections, medical debt, or hard inquiries needing active dispute help
People working toward a specific goal such as buying a car or qualifying for a mortgage
Entrepreneurs and small business owners seeking to build business credit or secure startup funding
Clients who prefer a personalized, relationship-based approach over automated credit repair software
diagnosis: Rapid Credit Boosters is best suited for motivated individuals or small business owners who want hands-on, goal-focused credit repair and are comfortable with a boutique firm rather than a large platform. The main caveat is significant: the business's operational status is uncertain as of early 2026, with a Yelp closure flag and no public pricing — prospective clients should call and independently verify the company is still active before paying anything.
services: Home buying and mortgage qualification assistance
Startup and small business funding preparation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 5755 Oberlin Dr UNIT 323, San Diego, CA 92121
